segments.text: Surely there is a mine for silver and a place where gold is refined.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Iron is taken from the earth, and copper is smelted from ore.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Man puts an end to the darkness; he probes the farthest recesses
for ore in deepest darkness.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Far from human habitation he cuts a shaftin places forgotten by the foot of man.
Far from men he dangles and sways.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Food may come from the earth, but from below it is transformed as by fire.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Its rocks are the source of sapphires, containing flecks of gold.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No bird of prey knows that path; no falcon’s eye has seen it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Proud beasts have never trodden it; no lion has ever prowled over it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The miner strikes the flint; he overturns mountains at their base.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He hews out channels in the rocks, and his eyes spot every treasure.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He stops up the sources of the streamsto bring what is hidden to light.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But where can wisdom be found, and where does understanding dwell?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No man can know its value, nor is it found in the land of the living.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The ocean depths say, ‘It is not in me,’while the sea declares, ‘It is not with me.’
segments.speaker: The depth

segments.text: It cannot be bought with gold, nor can its price be weighed out in silver.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir, in precious onyx or sapphire.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Neither gold nor crystal can compare to it, nor jewels of fine gold be exchanged for it.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Coral and quartz are unworthy of mention; the price of wisdom is beyond rubies.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Topaz from Cush cannot compare to it, nor can it be valued in pure gold.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: From where then does wisdom come, and where does understanding dwell?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: It is hidden from the eyes of every living thing and concealed from the birds of the air.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Abaddon and Death say,‘We have heard a rumor about it.’
segments.speaker: Destruction and death

segments.text: But God understands its way, and He knows its place.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For He looks to the ends of the earth and sees everything under the heavens.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: When God fixed the weight of the wind and measured out the waters,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: when He set a limit for the rain and a path for the thunderbolt,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: then He looked at wisdom and appraised it; He established it and searched it out.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
And He said to man, ‘Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom,
and to turn away from evil is understanding.’
segments.speaker: God
